ADDITIONAL INFORMATION FROM THE STORM SURVEY...WIDESPREAD TREE LIMB DAMAGE ACROSS THE EASTERN HALF OF ROTAN. A FEW TREES BLOWN OVER AS WELL. WINDS ESIMATED AT 60 TO 70 MPH.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND WIDESPREAD DAMAGE TO TREE LIMBS ACROSS THE EASTERN HALF OF ROTAN. A FEW TREES WERE BLOWN OVER AND SEVERAL LARGE STORAGE TANKS WERE ALSO KNOCKED OVER. IN ADDITION...MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED TO A FEW METAL ROOFS. WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 60 TO 70 MPH.
LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED FLOODING 8 INCHES OF WATER OVER THE ROAD ON FARM TO MARKET ROAD 2133... BETWEEN BALLINGER AND ROWENA.
LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED THAT HIGHWAY 83 ALONG THE BORDER OF RUNNELS AND CONCHO COUNTIES WAS CLOSED... WITH ABOUT 4 FEET OF WATER OVER THE ROADWAY.
LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT FROM RUNNELS COUNTY REPORTED A CLOSURE OF HIGHWAY 67 BETWEEN BALLINGER AND SAN ANGELO... DUE TO HIGH WATER.

THE TAYLOR COUNTY SHERIFF OFFICE REPORTED THE CLOSURE OF SEVERAL COUNTY ROADS...DUE TO HIGH WATER. THE LIST OF CLOSURES IS FOR THE FOLLOWING COUNTY ROADS... 126 127 150...154...353...AND 360. IN ADDITION...THE ABILENE POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED CONSIDERABLE STREET FLOODING. BARRICADES WERE PLACED ON SEVERAL OF THE FLOODED STREETS.
THE SAN ANGELO DPS REPORTED WATER OVER THE ROAD AT THE CONCHO RIVER BRIDGE...ON FARM TO MARKET ROAD 1692.

LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICIALS REPORTED A FEW HIGH WATER RESCUES DUE TO FLOODING ALONG THE SERVICE ROAD TO INTERSTATE 20...NEAR OPLIN. SEVERAL LOW WATER CROSSINGS HAVE ALSO BEEN FLOODED IN CALLAHAN COUNTY.
